title: Zaluzianskya africana
taxonomy: Zaluzianskya africana is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the night-scented flax. It is native to Southern Africa, and is a member of the genus Zaluzianskya. The genus Zaluzianskya is a group of flowering plants that are native to Africa, and are often referred to as night-scented plants. These plants are known for their strong, sweet fragrance that is released at night to attract pollinators. The classification of Zaluzianskya africana places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Zaluzianskya africana, also known as the evening stock, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of Africa.
